# Code of Conduct

This Code of Conduct defines the project behaviour expected in Planwright spaces.
- [Purpose](#purpose)
- [Expectations](#expectations)
- [Unacceptable Behaviour](#unacceptable-behaviour)
- [Scope](#scope)
- [Maintainer Responsibility](#maintainer-responsibility)
- [Reporting](#reporting)
- [Response](#response)
- [Notes](#notes)

## Purpose

Planwright is intended to be a serious infrastructure planning and review tool.

The purpose of this Code of Conduct is to keep project discussion, review and collaboration constructive, professional and usable for everyone involved.

## Expectations

Participants in the Planwright project are expected to:
- act in good faith
- communicate clearly and professionally
- criticise ideas, code and decisions without attacking people
- respect project scope, standards and review processes
- avoid disruptive, abusive or needlessly hostile behaviour
- accept that maintainers may make final decisions about project direction
- avoid making unsupported security, compliance or compatibility claims

## Unacceptable Behaviour

The following behaviour is not acceptable in project spaces:
- harassment or intimidation
- personal attacks
- discriminatory or degrading language
- bad-faith disruption
- repeated refusal to follow documented project processes
- intentionally misleading technical discussion
- publication of private information without consent
- public disclosure of suspected vulnerabilities before maintainers have had a reasonable chance to respond

## Scope

This Code of Conduct applies to:
- issues
- pull requests
- discussions
- review comments
- security-report handling where participants interact with maintainers
- other repository-associated project spaces

## Maintainer Responsibility

Maintainers are responsible for enforcing this Code of Conduct reasonably and proportionately.

They may:
- ask participants to change tone or behaviour
- edit, hide or remove content where project spaces allow it
- lock issues or discussions
- restrict participation
- decline contributions that repeatedly disregard project standards
- escalate security-sensitive reports to the private process in [`SECURITY.md`](SECURITY.md)

Enforcement should be practical and tied to keeping the project usable. It should not be used to silence good-faith technical disagreement.

## Reporting

Serious conduct concerns should be reported privately to `mail@steadytao.com`.

Reports should include:
- the project space where the issue happened
- links or screenshots where available
- a short description of what happened
- any immediate safety or privacy concern

Do not include secrets, credentials or vulnerability proof-of-concept material in a conduct report unless the security process asks for it.

## Response

Maintainers *(currently only @steadytao)* will review reports and choose a proportionate response.

Possible responses include:
- no action where the report is not supported
- a private warning
- a public moderation note
- removal or editing of content
- temporary or permanent participation limits
- referral to the security process where the issue involves vulnerability handling

## Notes

This Code of Conduct is intended to support a functional working environment, not performative process.

The project values direct technical discussion. Directness is not a licence for abusive, corrosive or misleading behaviour.
